metadata.purpose: Core-slim line-review ledger: every retained file, function, and public knob must be classified as keep, merge, delete, or extract-pr before the refactor PR is review-ready.
metadata.next_step: Execute plan_final.md file-level queues in order, starting with QI/device-QI artifacts and experimental solver route extraction, then RHSMode-1 orchestration collapse, operator cleanup, transport collapse, validation/evidence shrink, examples/tests curation, and root/API cleanup.
metadata.latest_tranche_evidence: Removed direct-tail support-mode preflight and stable-output plumbing; deleted 13 tracked generated performance-output JSON files; consolidated seven release/data/audit/hygiene scripts into validation CLIs; extracted unsupported single-case sharded and multi-GPU performance campaign examples/tests; extracted high-nu and W7-X publication-audit generators while retaining compact checked artifacts; extracted optional Lineax and Equinox/JAXopt adoption benchmark drivers; moved Krylov implementation from the package root to dkx.solvers.krylov while keeping dkx.solver as a compatibility alias; rewrote plan_final.md into a stricter finite work order and added rule-expanded whole-repo inventory coverage; deleted the duplicate root utils/ tree and promoted the enhanced noninteractive upstream utility behavior into examples/sfincs_examples/utils, leaving one canonical vendored utility location; promoted the remaining Python release/profiling scripts into dkx.validation modules so scripts/ is no longer a Python implementation area; merged validation figure-panel helpers into validation artifacts so evidence schemas and docs-panel gates have one owner; merged reduced and scaled suite runners into dkx.validation.suite with explicit reduced/scaled subcommands and legacy script-name dispatch for archived tests; consolidated production-input generation, README audit regeneration, and write-output tracing into dkx.validation.release subcommands so validation reaches five implementation modules plus __init__; removed unsupported BLR/HSS and nested-dissection frontal stable solver routes with their aliases, env knobs, metadata, tests, and docs; made plan_final.md line/file review criteria explicit for public symbols, env vars, CLI flags, namelist aliases, output keys, diagnostics fields, QI/device-QI artifacts, example folders, and test files. Focused local checks across those tranches covered source-tree/domain contracts, release/data/audit tests, scaled-suite source-tree checks, publication-audit extraction checks, package CLI checks, upstream utility smoke tests, Ruff, compileall, JSON validation, diff hygiene, and size guard.; extracted stable QI/device-QI generated artifacts, promotion tests, default validation references, and README/docs artifact claims from the stable core; compacted the research-lane manifest to existing small evidence and branch-scoped QI/device-QI as deferred research; removed four untested performance profiling/campaign scripts from stable examples and rewrote docs to point to retained output-format, structured-solve, transport-worker, and checked compile/runtime evidence instead; removed optional Optax calibration/geometry-fitting/publication-figure optimization scripts from stable examples and kept the documented optimization lane on tested QA objective, bootstrap-current comparison, and scan-promotion workflows; removed the W7-X high-nu candidate preconditioner harness and its direct test from stable examples while retaining the checked performance figure and full scan command; removed the long optimization promotion campaign wrapper from stable examples and documentation while retaining the explicit scan, audit, and comparison workflow; demoted unpromoted active native/symbolic RHSMode-1 preconditioners from automatic default ladders and updated docs/tests so stable auto starts from residual-clean Fortran-reduced active LU with guarded sparse/coarse fallbacks.; removed RHSMode-1 active-symbolic frontal/superblock/block-Schur/coupled-Schur wrapper routes, policy dataclasses, tests, and source-map entries while preserving the generic symbolic sparse backend for transport diagnostics.; removed disabled pre-Krylov x-block probe-coarse and disabled x-block post-solve correction routes, hidden env knobs, metadata/output fields, docs, and stale tests while preserving the live sparse-PC post-minres polish path; removed manual FP x-block global/high-x correction tail stages, env gates, metadata/output fields, public exports, and stale tests while keeping the supported sparse x-block rescue path; removed opt-in sparse sxblock rescue tail policy, driver branch, stage dataclasses/function, env knob, exports, and tests while preserving the stable sxblock_tz preconditioner; local evidence covered compileall, 436 focused policy/sparse-PC tests, Ruff, stale-reference search, and pending broader guards.; added the canonical root modules run.py (RHSMode-2/3 end-to-end driver: inputs -> drift_kinetic -> solve -> moments -> writer/console) and writer.py (canonical RHSMode-2/3 sfincsOutput writer) with no legacy problems/operators/outputs imports, pinned to the legacy transport pipeline and writer output by tests/test_run_transport.py at 1e-10; old-owner deletion for the RHSMode-3 slice follows as a separate reviewed step in the same series.; slice-1 deletion pass: deleted the superseded probing-based RHSMode-3 solvers/block_tridiagonal_transport.py POC (canonical solve.py tier-1 is the owner, now refereed directly against the recorded Fortran v3 stateVector fixtures) with its opt-in branch in problems/transport_solve.py and its test module; promoted the transport-matrix-v3 CLI subcommand and the bare-run/write-output dispatch for RHSMode 2/3 inputs onto the canonical dkx.run.run_transport_matrix stack, keeping the legacy outputs writer only for npz/export_f/solver-trace/no-overwrite options and RHSMode-1 runs; added a direct canonical-vs-Fortran transportMatrix parity assertion in tests/test_run_transport.py at the documented tolerances.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve sharded, multi-worker, multi-GPU, and long profiling campaigns outside the stable core; the legacy in-repo owners were deleted with the legacy pipeline.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: Serial/default CPU and GPU solves must remain available; scaling claims move out unless fresh evidence is retained.

extraction_groups.purpose: Preserve historical benchmark campaigns, publication figures, Zenodo audits, and long-run validation scripts outside the stable core.
extraction_groups.core_removal_gate: README/docs can keep small checked figures and summaries, but long-run campaign drivers leave the core tree.

rules.name: repo_metadata_and_top_level_docs
rules.next_action: Scrub public prose after source and evidence decisions settle.

rules.name: github_ci_workflows
rules.line_target: keep only package, docs, coverage, and publish workflows
rules.next_action: Keep CI under 10 minutes while preserving coverage and docs gates.

rules.name: package_source
rules.line_target: <=50 Python files and <=50k package lines unless ledger exception
rules.next_action: Continue solver research after BLR/HSS and nested-dissection stable-route removal: remove or extract multifrontal/true-operator rescue/manual native-factor routes that are not auto-admitted, then collapse RHSMode-1 policy tables.

rules.name: compact_test_fixtures
rules.line_target: compact JSON only; large data belongs in release assets
rules.next_action: Retain fixtures only when a physics/API/docs claim test consumes them.

rules.name: reference_fixture_data
rules.line_target: deduplicate into compact fixtures or release-hosted assets
rules.next_action: Delete unused reference blobs and keep only data needed for stable claims.

rules.name: generated_suite_reports
rules.line_target: move long-run reports to release assets or research PRs
rules.next_action: Keep only compact claim fixtures required by current docs/readme.

rules.name: reduced_test_inputs
rules.line_target: minimal representative inputs; no historical duplicates
rules.next_action: Parametrize tests over fewer canonical input fixtures.

rules.name: python_tests
rules.proof_owner: pytest coverage and domain regression suites
rules.next_action: Delete tests that only pin extracted research paths; merge the retained tests into parametrized physics, numerics, API, autodiff, parity, and docs-claim suites.

rules.name: docs_static_evidence
rules.proof_owner: docs-claim tests and validation figure schema tests
rules.line_target: compressed checked figures and compact JSON only
rules.next_action: Delete stale campaign artifacts and regenerate retained figures from stable workflows.

rules.name: upstream_docs_sources
rules.line_target: retain only cited source material needed for stable docs
rules.next_action: Move bulky or uncited upstream material to external references.

rules.name: public_docs
rules.line_target: standalone user docs with no branch-history prose
rules.next_action: Scrub unsupported research claims after code extraction.

rules.name: original_sfincs_examples
rules.line_target: retain the single upstream-style v3 reference tree needed for parity examples
rules.next_action: Keep as reference inputs; do not add generated outputs. The duplicate examples/upstream tree was deleted from stable examples.

rules.name: publication_and_performance_examples
rules.line_target: move long generators/campaigns out of stable examples
rules.next_action: Move long publication/performance generators to preservation branches; keep only compact checked figures and their source data when public docs still cite them.

rules.name: optimization_examples
rules.line_target: at most one curated optimization workflow in stable examples
rules.next_action: Extract unpromoted campaigns; keep one pedagogical optimization example.

rules.name: curated_examples
rules.proof_owner: examples tree contract and smoke-free API tests
rules.line_target: <=10 curated workflows outside original v3 references
rules.next_action: Reduce to original v3 references plus no more than 10 teaching workflows: CLI solve/plot, Python solve, output formats, transport, bootstrap/Redl, ambipolar root, autodiff, geometry loading, optimization, and validation comparison.

rules.name: temporary_scripts
rules.proof_owner: script import and release/data CLI tests
rules.line_target: empty or documented release/data tooling only
rules.next_action: Convert useful scripts into package CLIs/tests/examples; delete the rest.

rules.name: legacy_utils
rules.next_action: Delete obsolete upstream utilities or move any documented user workflow into examples.

rules.name: evidence_tooling
rules.next_action: Keep tools/ to the reference-data generator and benchmark drivers promoted by plan_final.md step 4; delete anything unused by CI or the README tables.

entries.retention_reason: The canonical matrix-free v3 drift-kinetic operator owns every physics term family; it is the single operator owner after the legacy stack deletion.
entries.next_step: Keep term additions inside this owner; split only with a physics-family boundary.

entries.retention_reason: The three-tier canonical solve policy (block-tridiagonal, GCROT, direct) is the single solver owner after the legacy solvers package deletion.
entries.next_step: Keep tier policies here; new tiers need parity and memory gates.

entries.retention_reason: The canonical sfincsOutput writer owns every emitted dataset family; it replaced the legacy outputs package writer pipeline at the purge.
entries.next_step: Keep dataset families here; new fields need golden-based referees.

entries.retention_reason: The consolidated flux-surface geometry owner covers analytic, VMEC, Boozer, and Fourier constructors; it replaced the legacy geometry package.
entries.next_step: Keep constructors here; new geometry sources need Fortran goldens.

entries.retention_reason: The canonical moment/diagnostic integrals (fluxes, flows, NTV, classical transport, transport matrices) are the single output-physics owner.
entries.next_step: Keep moment formulas here with golden-based referee coverage.

entries.retention_reason: Release/benchmark orchestration is core validation tooling but keeps accumulating wrappers; trim toward the documented release gates only.
entries.next_step: Remove wrappers whose lanes were retired with the legacy stack.

entries.retention_reason: Parallel scaling campaigns are not required for the slim core CLI/API and remain research/performance infrastructure.
entries.next_step: Extract long-run worker/sharded paths; keep only any proven simple CPU/GPU backend defaults.

entries.retention_reason: Small validation fixture checks and docs-panel evidence builders are core; research-lane, benchmark, and publication campaign validators should move out.
entries.next_step: Keep schema checks and compact figure-panel evidence used by retained docs/tests; extract publication/benchmark campaign validators and reduce this owner below 900 lines.

entries.retention_reason: Examples are user education, but the release core should contain only Fortran-v3 references and at most 10 curated workflows.
entries.next_step: Move performance, publication, QI, and one-off audits to research PRs or release assets.

entries.retention_reason: Tests must prove the slim core, not preserve every historical experiment.
entries.next_step: Reorganize into unit, physics, regression, cli_io, fixtures, and integration_optional after extraction.

entries.retention_reason: Python implementation scripts are not part of the stable architecture; scripts may contain only documented shell or release-maintenance tooling.
entries.next_step: Keep the Fortran MPI shell wrapper only while documented, otherwise move shell release tooling into docs or delete scripts/ entirely.

entries.retention_reason: Stable performance examples keep output-format, JIT, transport-worker, structured-solve, and bounded parity/runtime evidence; unsupported sharded, multi-GPU, and optional ecosystem adoption drivers have been extracted.
entries.next_step: Continue shrinking performance examples toward one output-format workflow plus one documented transport-worker benchmark; remaining long profiling drivers should become package validation tooling or research-lane material.

entries.retention_reason: Publication figure regeneration is valuable but not required in the slim user-facing core.
entries.next_step: Move figure generation campaigns to publication-audits PR and keep checked final figures only if small.
